qa_pairs.question_text: Jensen, the Blackwell ramp is clearly impressive. Can you give us a sense for what percentage of your largest hyperscale customers have actually deployed Blackwell in production versus running pilot workloads? And what does the visibility look like for the next-generation Rubin platform?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Vivek, all five of our largest hyperscale customers are in production with Blackwell at meaningful scale today. Four of the five have already placed firm orders for Rubin systems that begin shipping in the second half of calendar 2026. We have lead times stretching well into calendar 2027 for Rubin, which gives us strong visibility into next fiscal year demand. We will share more detail on the Rubin platform at our GTC conference in March.

qa_pairs.question_text: Colette, the gross margin commentary is helpful but I want to push a little. Can you break out specifically what is helping margin in the second half, and how investors should think about Rubin economics in the first year of the ramp given typical new-platform headwinds?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Stacy, the second half tailwinds are improving yields on the GB200 and GB300 SKUs and a richer mix of higher-margin networking content per system. I am not going to give you a specific Rubin gross-margin number today. What I will say is that we have designed Rubin with the lessons learned from Blackwell, and we expect the new-platform margin trough to be shallower and shorter than what we experienced last cycle.

qa_pairs.question_text: There has been a lot of investor focus on the AI infrastructure investments by sovereign nations. Can you size that opportunity for us and tell us what percentage of Q2 data center revenue came from sovereign-AI customers?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Joe, sovereign AI is now one of our fastest-growing customer categories. We are working with national initiatives in over 30 countries, and we expect sovereign AI to contribute more than 25 billion dollars of cumulative revenue over the next 24 months. I am not going to give you the exact Q2 percentage breakout today.

qa_pairs.question_text: On the China situation, the export controls have been a headwind for several quarters now. Can you give us an update on the H20 successor product timing and your latest read on Chinese demand?
qa_pairs.answer_text: CJ, we are continuing to work with the US government to ensure our products comply with export rules while still serving the Chinese market. The next-generation product designed for that market is in advanced development. We expect to be in a position to ship that product in the first quarter of calendar 2026. Chinese demand remains strong but we are not embedding meaningful China revenue in our Q3 or Q4 guidance until we have clarity on the shipment timeline.

qa_pairs.question_text: Jensen, you mentioned Spectrum-X exceeded 2.6 billion in the quarter. Can you give us a longer-term view on networking attach rates and where you expect Spectrum-X plus NVLink Switch to land as a percentage of system revenue by exit fiscal 2027?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Tim, networking attach has been climbing every quarter. In the Blackwell generation customers are buying significantly more networking content per GPU than they did in Hopper, and that trend accelerates with Rubin given the rack-scale architecture. We expect networking to represent over 22 percent of data center system revenue by exit fiscal 2027, up from roughly 14 percent today.

qa_pairs.question_text: Colette, on operating expenses, you guided 4.1 billion for the quarter. How should we think about opex growth in the second half given the R&D ramp for Rubin and the cadence of new product launches?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Aaron, we expect non-GAAP operating expense to grow in the high teens sequentially in Q3 as we ramp investment ahead of the Rubin launch. We will give updated full-year color when we report Q3 results.

qa_pairs.question_text: On enterprise versus hyperscale mix, can you talk about how enterprise AI adoption is progressing and whether the enterprise pipeline is large enough to be material in the next 12 months?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Atif, enterprise is one of the bright spots. Enterprise AI revenue more than tripled year over year in Q2, though it is still a smaller share of total data center than hyperscale. We have over 12,000 enterprises now in active deployment of NVIDIA AI Enterprise software, up from roughly 4,500 a year ago. We expect enterprise AI to exceed 10 percent of data center revenue by the end of fiscal 2026.

qa_pairs.question_text: Jensen, on supply, you mentioned visibility through the second half. Are there specific component constraints we should be aware of, and is HBM capacity still the binding constraint?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Harlan, HBM remains tight but we have secured supply commitments from all three of our HBM partners that cover our build plan through the first half of calendar 2027. The current binding constraint in our build is actually advanced packaging capacity at TSMC. We are working closely with TSMC on capacity expansion and expect packaging capacity to roughly double by the middle of calendar 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Q3 fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of 52 billion dollars plus or minus 2 percent.
commitments.source_quote: For Q3 we are guiding revenue of 52 billion dollars plus or minus 2 percent.

commitments.commitment_text: Rubin systems begin shipping in the second half of calendar 2026.
commitments.source_quote: Four of the five have already placed firm orders for Rubin systems that begin shipping in the second half of calendar 2026.

commitments.source_quote: we expect sovereign AI to contribute more than 25 billion dollars of cumulative revenue over the next 24 months.

commitments.source_quote: We expect networking to represent over 22 percent of data center system revenue by exit fiscal 2027, up from roughly 14 percent today.
